UX2 Sandbox — User Guide

Copilot & Agents Testing Environment

Welcome to the UX2 Sandbox

This is a safe, isolated testing environment where you can explore Microsoft Copilot and its AI agents. Nothing you do here affects any live systems — feel free to experiment.

How are you accessing the sandbox?

Choose your access method below to see the relevant steps.

🌐

Remote — Personal Device

Using your own laptop or desktop with the guest credentials you've been given.

💻

In-Person — Sandbox Laptop

Using one of the pre-configured laptops that's already logged in and ready to go.

1

Open an Incognito / Private window

This ensures you don't clash with any existing Microsoft accounts you may be signed into on your regular browser.

  • Chrome: Ctrl + Shift + N  (Mac: ⌘ + Shift + N)
  • Edge: Ctrl + Shift + N  (Mac: ⌘ + Shift + N)
2

Navigate to Microsoft 365

In the address bar of your incognito window, type the URL below and press Enter.

https://microsoft365.com
💡 Click the URL above to copy it to your clipboard.
3

Sign in with your sandbox credentials

Use the guest account credentials that have been shared with you. These are separate from your normal work or personal Microsoft accounts — that's expected.

  • Enter the email address provided to you
  • Enter the password provided to you
  • If prompted for MFA or to "stay signed in", follow the on-screen instructions
⚠️ If you see a "pick an account" screen, make sure you choose the sandbox account — not your personal or work account.
4

You're in — welcome to the Copilot home screen

After signing in, you'll land on the Microsoft 365 Copilot home screen. This is your starting point for everything. Head to the "Using Copilot" tab above to learn what you can do from here.

1

Open the laptop

The sandbox laptops are already signed into a test account — you don't need any credentials. Just open the lid and you should see the Windows desktop or lock screen.

💡 If the laptop asks for a Windows PIN or password, ask the sandbox admin for help.
2

Open the browser and go to Microsoft 365

Open Edge (or any browser) and navigate to:

https://microsoft365.com

You should be automatically signed in. If you land on the Copilot home screen, you're good to go.

3

Ready to explore

That's it — you're in. Head to the "Using Copilot" tab above to learn how to navigate the interface.

The Copilot Home Screen

What you'll see

The Copilot home screen is your central hub. From here you can chat directly with Copilot, access AI agents, and launch Microsoft 365 apps like Teams and Outlook.

Chatting with Copilot

The main area in the centre of the screen is a chat interface. You can type questions, prompts, or requests directly — just like chatting with a colleague. Try things like:

  • "Summarise the latest emails in my inbox"
  • "Draft a project status update"
  • "What meetings do I have this week?"
🧪 This is a sandbox — there's no real data here. Copilot may respond with placeholder or empty results, and that's completely fine. The goal is to explore the interface and capabilities.

The Left-Hand Sidebar

The sidebar on the left is your navigation panel. It contains two key sections:

  • Agents — Browse, add, and build AI agents (see the Agents tab above for more)
  • Apps — Access Microsoft 365 apps like Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and more (see the Apps & Tools tab)

Working with AI Agents

Agents are specialised AI assistants built on top of Copilot. Some are pre-built by Microsoft, and you can also create your own.

1

Browsing available agents

In the left-hand sidebar, you'll see a list of agents. Click "All Agents" to open the Agent Store, where you can browse the full catalogue of Microsoft-built agents available in the sandbox.

From this view, you can read descriptions of each agent and add the ones you'd like to test to your sidebar for quick access.

2

Using an agent

Once you've added an agent, click on it in the sidebar to open a chat with that specific agent. Each agent has a focused purpose — for example, one might specialise in writing, another in data analysis.

Just chat with it as you would with Copilot, but expect responses tailored to that agent's area of expertise.

3

Building your own agent

Click "New Agent" in the left sidebar to open Agent Builder — a simplified interface that lets you create your own custom agent without writing any code.

You can define what the agent does, give it instructions, set its tone, and even connect it to specific data sources or actions.

💡 Building an agent is a great way to understand how these tools could work for your team. Try creating one that answers questions about a specific topic or process.

Accessing Microsoft 365 Apps

Finding the full app list

At the bottom of the left-hand sidebar, click on Apps, then select "More Apps" to see the full list of Microsoft 365 applications available in the sandbox.

Available apps

From the full app list, you can launch apps like:

  • Teams — messaging, meetings, and collaboration
  • Outlook — email and calendar
  • Word / Excel / PowerPoint — document creation with Copilot built in
  • OneDrive — file storage
  • SharePoint — team sites and content management
🧪 Each of these apps has Copilot integrated. For example, in Word you can ask Copilot to draft content; in Excel you can ask it to analyse data. Explore freely!

Copilot Cowork

Copilot Cowork is a new experience within Microsoft 365 Copilot that acts as a persistent, always-on AI collaborator. Unlike standard Copilot chat — which is session-based — Cowork runs alongside you as you work, proactively surfacing suggestions, drafting content, and helping you manage tasks across M365 apps.

🚧 Testing in progress: Copilot Cowork is currently undergoing evaluation and review by the UX2 team. If you encounter any issues, bugs, or unexpected behaviour, please reach out to us directly.
⚠️ Usage limits: Please note that Cowork has usage limits in place. Keep this in mind during your testing — if you hit a limit, you may need to wait before continuing.

Accessing Cowork

1

Open Copilot

Navigate to the Copilot home screen as usual (via microsoft365.com or the app on the sandbox laptop). Cowork should be pinned in the left-hand sidebar, ready to go.

2

Open Cowork

Click on Cowork in the sidebar to open the Cowork home screen. From here you can start interacting with your AI collaborator.

3

Choose your model

Cowork lets you select which AI model powers your session. You can pick from:

  • Auto — lets Cowork choose the best model for the task (recommended for general use)
  • Sonnet — faster responses, good for straightforward tasks
  • Opus — more capable, better for complex reasoning and longer outputs

You can switch models at any time using the model selector in the interface.

Use Cases to Try

Here are some things worth exploring during your testing:

Drafting & writing assistance

Ask Cowork to help you draft emails, reports, summaries, or other written content. Try giving it context about your audience and tone to see how it adapts.

Research & analysis

Ask Cowork to research a topic, compare options, or break down a complex subject. It can pull from web sources and reason through multi-step questions.

Working across M365 apps

Test how Cowork interacts with other apps in the ecosystem — can it help you with data in Excel, draft slides in PowerPoint, or summarise content from Teams and Outlook?

Comparing models

Try running the same prompt on different models (Sonnet vs Opus) to see how the outputs differ in quality, depth, and speed. This is valuable feedback for the UX2 team.

💡 Take note of anything that surprises you — both positively and negatively. Your feedback helps shape how these tools get rolled out more broadly.

Frequently Asked Questions

Your browser may already be signed into your personal or work Microsoft account. Incognito mode starts a clean session so you can log in with the sandbox credentials without any conflicts.
No. This sandbox runs on a completely separate Azure tenant with no connection to any enterprise environment. Nothing you do here touches real data, users, or systems.
Nope, that's expected. Since this is a clean test environment, there aren't real emails, files, or calendar events for Copilot to pull from. The goal is to explore the interface, test prompts, and understand the capabilities — not to work with real data.
You have a standard (non-admin) account, so some admin-level settings will be locked. But you can freely explore all the apps, agents, and Copilot features available to you. If you need something enabled, contact the sandbox admin.
Just open a new incognito window and sign in again with your sandbox credentials. Sessions may time out after a period of inactivity — this is normal.
Reach out to the sandbox admin who provided your credentials. They can troubleshoot access issues, reset passwords, or enable additional features.
